summary: Tested User Activity Log feature - audit trail tracking all key user actions. All 10 backend API tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed - Activity Log page accessible from sidebar, shows filter controls (User, Category, Action, Date), table with proper columns, filter dropdowns populate dynamically, Apply/Reset buttons work correctly.
passed_tests: BACKEND: GET /api/activity-logs returns activity log entries (list)
BACKEND: GET /api/activity-logs?username=admin filters by user correctly
BACKEND: GET /api/activity-logs?category=auth filters by category correctly
BACKEND: GET /api/activity-logs?action=login filters by action correctly
BACKEND: GET /api/activity-logs/categories returns filter options (categories, usernames, actions)
BACKEND: Login action creates activity log entry with action=login, category=auth
BACKEND: Activity logs endpoints require authentication (401/403 without token)
BACKEND: User toggle (disable/enable) creates activity log with category=user
BACKEND: Activity logs sorted by timestamp descending (newest first)
BACKEND: Activity logs support date range filtering (start_date, end_date)
FRONTEND: Activity Log link visible in sidebar navigation
FRONTEND: Activity Log page loads with proper title and entry count
FRONTEND: Filter controls present (User, Category, Action, From date, To date)
FRONTEND: Table has all required columns (Timestamp, User, Action, Category, Details)
FRONTEND: Filter dropdowns populate dynamically from /api/activity-logs/categories
FRONTEND: Apply filter button works and refreshes data
FRONTEND: Reset filter button clears all filters
FRONTEND: Combined filters work correctly (username + category)
FRONTEND: Action badges have proper color styling (login=green, deleted=red, enabled=green, disabled=amber)
FRONTEND: Category badges have proper color styling (auth=blue, user=purple)
critical_code_review_comments: Activity logger implementation is clean and reusable - log_activity() function properly inserts into activity_logs collection
Activity routes properly exclude MongoDB _id field from responses
Filter options endpoint returns sorted distinct values for categories, usernames, and actions
Frontend ActivityLog.js properly uses data-testid attributes for all interactive elements
Color coding for actions and categories enhances readability
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_activity_log.py - Created comprehensive test suite for Activity Log feature
seed_data_creation: Test user TEST_activity_user created and cleaned up automatically by test suite
context_for_next_testing_agent: Activity Log feature fully functional. Key endpoints: GET /api/activity-logs (with optional filters: username, category, action, start_date, end_date), GET /api/activity-logs/categories (returns distinct values for filter dropdowns). Activity logs are created by log_activity() function called from auth_routes.py (login, user CRUD, toggle-status), batch_routes.py (batch CRUD), dispatch_routes.py, finished_product_routes.py, semi_finished_routes.py. Frontend page at /activity-log shows filterable table with color-coded action/category badges. All data-testid attributes properly implemented.

features: Filter controls (User, Category, Action, Date range)
Table with Timestamp, User, Action, Category, Details columns
